description: Letter from Meir b. Hillel b. Sadoq to a prominent figure in Fustat, probably Sar Shalom ha-Levi Gaon. The identification of the writer is based on his style and handwriting. The writer is hesitant in accepting a post he was granted in Alexandria because it would entail parting from his son. The letter mentions one of the most famous jurists of Alexandria, the faqīh Ibn ʿAwf. Frenkel identifies this man with the Mālikī jurist Abū Ṭāhir Ismāʿīl b. Makkī b. ʿĪsā b. ʿAwf of Alexandria (d. 1188), who was one of Saladin's teachers. The same faqīh is also mentioned in T-S 12.290 and T-S 16.272. Dating: Before 1188 CE, based on the identification of the faqīh. The letter also mentions a blind person, a 'rabbenu ha-qodesh', Alexandria, as well as ahl al-Maḥalla.
description_he: מכתב ממאיר בן הלל בן צדוק לאציל בפוסטאט, על היסוסיו לקבל תפקיד באלכסנדריה, מזכיר פקיה אבן עוף
